bonuses.leadin: In her essay “Professions for Women,” Virginia Woolf admits that she was so tormented by “the shadow of [this figure’s] wings” and “the rustling of her skirts” that “at last I killed her.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this conceptual figure who embodies an ideal of Victorian womanhood. This figure titles a narrative poem by Coventry Patmore, which was inspired by the devotion of his wife Emily Augusta.
This author’s essay “The Extinct Angel” describes how the race of the angel in the house has died out. This author also wrote the story “The Yellow Wallpaper.”

Elaine Showalter’s essay “Killing the Angel in the House” examines the sexist backlash against a writer with this surname for her novel The Group. Another writer with this surname created John Grady Cole, who travels to Mexico to become a rancher.
answers: McCarthy [accept Mary McCarthy or Cormac McCarthy] (The second sentence is from All the Pretty Horses.)
answers_sanitized: the angel in the house [prompt on angel]
McCarthy [accept Mary McCarthy or Cormac McCarthy] (The second sentence is from All the Pretty Horses.)

bonuses.leadin: In this novel, Gene Harrogate was sent to a work camp after he “violated” a farmer’s watermelons, which led to his nickname of “the Moonlight Melonmounter.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which Cornelius, the title character, leaves his luxurious life to live in solitude as a catfish-seller in Knoxville along the Tennessee River.
This author wrote the semi-autobiographical Suttree, in addition to a novel featuring psychopath hitman Anton Chigurh, No Country for Old Men, and the post-apocalyptic novel The Road.
In this other McCarthy novel, John Grady romances Alejandra while working on a ranch after escaping from the people pursuing Jimmy Blevins.

bonuses.leadin: Along with The Crossing and Cities of the Plain, it makes up its author’s Border Trilogy. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this book which revolves around the travels and adventures of Lacey Rawlins and John Grady Cole, the latter of whom proposes to Alejandra, whose father betrays them to the authorities.
Another work about horses is this 1973 play by Peter Shaffer is about Martin Dysart’s attempts to cure Alan Strang of his obsession with horses.
A book about Jody Tiflin’s life in the west, The Red Pony, was written by this author who often used ranches in novels such as Of Mice and Men and East of Eden.

bonuses.leadin: The main character of this novel begins an affair with Alejandra before Jimmy Blevins is hanged. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel, in which Lacey Rawlins accompanies John Grady to Mexico. It precedes The Crossing in its author’s “Border Trilogy.”
All the Pretty Horses was written by this American author of violent modern Westerns such as Blood Meridian and No Country for Old Men.

McCarthy also created this character, who chooses to spare the wounded man Shelby and refuses to abandon Sam Tate. At the end of the novel he appears in, he tells his antagonist “You ain’t nothin” before being killed.
